role: Secretary-General of the Democratic Party of Iranian Kurdistan
bio: Sadegh Sharafkandi (1938–1992) was a Kurdish physician and political leader who served as Secretary-General of the [Democratic Party of Iranian Kurdistan](/orgs/democratic-party-of-iranian-kurdistan) from 1989 until his assassination. He assumed the leadership after the [assassination of Abdul Rahman Ghassemlou](/events/ghassemlou-assassination-1989) in Vienna. On 17 September 1992, Sharafkandi and three companions were shot dead at the Mykonos restaurant in Berlin by agents linked to the Iranian government. The subsequent German trial (1993–1997) produced a landmark verdict directly implicating senior Iranian leaders — including [Ali Khamenei](/people/ali-khamenei), [Akbar Hashemi Rafsanjani](/people/akbar-hashemi-rafsanjani), and [Ali Fallahian](/people/ali-fallahian) — in ordering the killings, triggering the recall of all EU ambassadors from Tehran.
editorial_notes: Born in Mahabad. Assassinated 17 September 1992 in Berlin (Mykonos restaurant). Successor to Ghassemlou as PDKI leader.
